title: Tanker report - Week 13
summary:
Clean
The clean product freight market has been this week with LRs losing some of their usual alignment. TC1 has softened a touch down to WS180.63 (-WS3.43) with a TC20 run west dipping from $4,650,000 to $4,540,000. However, on the LR1s TC5 has climbed up 22.93 points to WS206.79 off the back of plenty of activity midweek.

children.title: Clean
children.summary: The clean product freight market has been this week with LRs losing some of their usual alignment. TC1 has softened a touch down to WS180.63 (-WS3.43) with a TC20 run west dipping from $4,650,000 to $4,540,000.

children.title: VLCC
children.summary: The VLCC market continued to fall this week. For the 270,000mt Middle East Gulf to China voyage the rate fell a further 10 points to WS80.05.

children.title: Suezmax
children.summary: The Black Sea and Mediterranean markets have been a little quieter this week, with the only notable fixture from CPC being an Italian charterer reported fixing a Greek owner 135,000mt at WS167.5 with sentiment a little softer. Accordingly, the rate for 135,000mt CPC/Augusta was assessed on Thursday 15 points lower than a week ago at WS166.33 (a round-trip TCE of $86,800 per day).

children.title: Aframax
children.summary: In the North Sea market, the rate for the 80,000mt Hound Point/Wilhelmshaven route settled about 1.5 points lower than a week ago at WS180 (a round-trip daily TCE of $75,800). In the Mediterranean, the rate for 80,000mt Ceyhan/Lavera fell back after the recent sharp rise, losing nearly 36 points to be rated at a little over WS245 (a daily round trip TCE of $94,100).
